message:
security: add rate limiting and fix cryptographic bias in share links

This commit addresses multiple security concerns in the share link system:

**Rate Limiting (route.ts, verify/route.ts)**
- Add in-memory rate limiting to GET /api/share/[code] endpoint:
  30 requests/minute per IP to prevent link enumeration attacks
- Add stricter rate limiting to POST /api/share/[code]/verify:
  5 attempts per 15 minutes per IP+code combination to thwart
  brute-force password attacks on protected links
- Both implementations include automatic bucket pruning and proper
  Retry-After headers for 429 responses

**Cryptographic Improvements (share-link-store.ts)**
- Fix modulo bias in link code generation: the previous implementation
  used `byte % 57` which creates ~3% bias toward certain characters
  (since 256 doesn't divide evenly by 57)
- Implement rejection sampling: only use bytes 0-227 (divisible by 57)
  and discard 228-255, ensuring uniform distribution across all chars
- Generate extra bytes to compensate for rejected values

**Search Engine Defensive Fix (engine.ts)**
- Replace map() with flatMap() in search result mapping to gracefully
  handle edge cases where a prompt ID in the match list might not exist
  in the prompts map (shouldn't happen, but defense-in-depth)

Security trade-off documented: GET endpoint reveals link existence via
timing, but this is intentional UX. The 57^12 code entropy combined with
rate limiting makes enumeration impractical.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
